evidence.text: Rocky's Best Friend/Lover/Girlfriend... Leonardo's Best Friend/Lover/Girlfriend...

editableNewDialogue.text: Can we do a quick recap before the next episode? I’m a little fuzzy on what triggered the last blinding light.

Sure. The group was fighting unusually strong monsters; Rocky, Leonardo, and Michelangelo were badly injured, and as Donatello, Ami, and the others pushed back in desperation, a blinding light engulfed everyone—suggesting an external force (artifact, portal, or unknown ally/enemy) intervened at the critical moment.
What are three possible explanations for that light that would fit the Sailor Moon vibe?

1) A protective resonance from the Silver Crystal reacting to mortal danger.
2) A time-space correction: Sailor Pluto (or a similar guardian force) briefly stabilizes the timeline.
3) A Dark Kingdom trap-teleport: the enemy yanks them away to separate, study, or bargain.
Pick one and write a short scene that doesn’t start a full episode yet—just the immediate aftermath.

Narrator: The white glare thinned like mist, leaving the world washed in pale color. Sound returned in pieces—distant wind, strained breathing, a soft crackle of fading energy.

Usagi: Rocky! Stay with me—please.

Rocky: (weakly) I’m… here. Just—can’t move much.

Ami: (kneeling beside Leonardo, steady but shaken) His pulse is fast. He lost a lot of strength in that last exchange.

Leonardo: (trying to sit up, failing) Everyone… okay?

Rei: Don’t talk, idiot. Save it.

Michelangelo: (grimacing, attempting a joke that doesn’t land) Next time… we celebrate after… the not-getting-stabbed part.

Minako: (looking around) This isn’t the cave. Where did it take us?

Donatello: (scanning with a handheld device) Energy signature is… unfamiliar. Not purely Dark Kingdom. Not purely Silver Crystal either.

Narrator: The air smelled like rain on stone, but there was no sky—only a vaulted darkness above them, dotted with faint, floating motes of light, as if stars had fallen indoors.

Ami: Those particles… they’re suspended, not drifting. Like they’re being held in place.

Rei: By what?

Donatello: By a field. Someone designed this.

Usagi: (tightening her grip on Rocky’s hand) Then whoever did it… they saved us. Right?

Narrator: No one answered. Because in the quiet between breaths, a second presence made itself known—slow footsteps, calm, unhurried… approaching from the dark.
For Episode 7, keep the Narrator style again, but don’t make it too long—more like a quick episode. Also, can you make sure Donatello doesn’t get hurt this time?

Narrator: After the chaos of the last battle, the team tries to settle into a calmer rhythm—but danger never stays away for long.

Donatello: (checking his gear) I’ll stay back and run support. No heroics from me today.

Minako: Good. I’m not letting anything touch you.

Narrator: The others brace themselves as strange sounds echo nearby, and a fresh wave of monsters creeps out of the shadows.

Usagi: Here they come—everyone, positions!

Leonardo: We end this fast.

Narrator: The fight is sharp and quick. Ami and Rei cover the front while Rocky and Michelangelo drive the monsters back. Donatello stays behind the line, tossing out devices and callouts, keeping the team one step ahead.

Donatello: Left side—now! That one’s baiting you!

Narrator: With a final coordinated strike, the monsters scatter and vanish, leaving only silence and heavy breathing.

Minako: (checking Donatello over) See? Not a scratch.

Donatello: (relieved) Best plan we’ve had all week.

Narrator: For once, the victory doesn’t come with a price—and the team takes the small win, together.
Hold up—small correction for the story going forward. I don’t want the romances to be Rocky/Usagi, Leo/Ami, Mikey/Rei, and Donnie/Minako anymore.

Narrator: The team’s victories had been loud, but the next step was quieter—because sometimes the biggest changes aren’t battles at all.

Rocky: (scratching the back of his neck) So… we’re keeping this strictly friends, right?

Usagi: (nods, soft but firm) Yeah. Friends. No “lover” stuff.

Leonardo: That works for me.

Ami: Same here. Partners in the fight, not couples.

Michelangelo: (relieved) Thank you. I’m not trying to date anybody in the middle of monster attacks.

Rei: Good. Then we’re all on the same page.

Donatello: And Minako—

Minako: (cuts in with a grin) Donnie, we’re friends. Teammates. That’s it.

Narrator: With the air cleared, the group refocused—no pairing pressure, no confessions—just allies trying to survive the next wave.
